name: The State Bank
description_short: The State Bank is an Iowa-chartered community bank serving Spirit Lake and Terril with full-service banking, loans, and investment services since 1928.
description_long: The State Bank of Terril was chartered on October 13, 1928, and is the oldest continuous charter in Dickinson County. The bank charter was moved to Spirit Lake in 1965 while retaining a branch in Terril, and the institution was renamed The State Bank to reflect its service to both communities. Today, the bank operates as an independent, locally-owned and operated commercial bank with a stated commitment to combining modern technology with personalized service. The State Bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking services. These include checking and savings accounts, online and mobile banking with deposit capture, bill pay, ATM/debit cards with chip technology, credit cards, mortgage loans with online application, consumer and commercial loans, insurance products, and investment services including annuities. The bank provides 24-hour banking access and has deployed mobile apps for both iPhone and Android platforms. They also offer calculators for loans, investments, annuities, and deposit insurance estimation. The State Bank distinguishes itself as a locally-owned independent bank rather than a chain institution. The website emphasizes the "personal touch" and community-focused operations, positioning the bank as an alternative to larger institutions. As of December 31, 2025, the bank reports total assets of $88.8 million, deposits of $81.1 million, and loans of $42.8 million, indicating a stable mid-sized community bank. As a traditional regional bank, The State Bank serves primarily the Spirit Lake and Terril, Iowa communities with limited geographic reach. The institution provides standard banking services but lacks the extensive branch networks, specialized products, or premium wealth management services of larger banks. Customers seeking out-of-state or nationwide banking presence may find geographic limitations restrictive.
services: Online banking with login and enrollment
Mobile banking apps for iPhone and Android
Financial calculators (loan, investment, annuity, deposit insurance)
pros: Oldest continuous bank charter in Dickinson County (since 1928) with 97+ years of stability
Locally owned and operated independent bank with decision-making focused on community benefit
Full-service banking including online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit, and 24-hour access
Mortgage application available online with ability to save and complete applications later
Dual branch locations in Spirit Lake and Terril for convenient access in both communities
Comprehensive digital services including mobile apps for iPhone and Android with bill pay capability
Investment and insurance services available in addition to traditional banking products
cons: Geographic service area limited to Spirit Lake and Terril, Iowa communities only
Small institution ($88.8M in assets) with limited resources compared to regional or national banks
No mention of deposit insurance beyond the estimator tool; FDIC status not explicitly stated on website
Limited product information available; specific interest rates, fees, and terms not disclosed on website
best_for: Residents of Spirit Lake and Terril, Iowa seeking personalized community banking relationships
Local businesses and agricultural operations in rural Dickinson County needing commercial banking services
Customers prioritizing personal service and local decision-making over large institution convenience
diagnosis: The State Bank is best for rural Iowa residents in Spirit Lake and Terril seeking traditional community banking with personalized service and local ownership. The main caveat is its limited geographic footprint and small asset base, making it suitable only for those in its service area who value local relationships over expansive branch networks or specialized financial services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
